summary: Large sysdolphin baselib particle display decomp slice for psdisp: adds implementations for particleSort and psDispParticles, types several psdisp globals, updates particleSort's public prototype, and expands HSD_psAppSRT padding into explicit float fields. Bot report records psdisp text progress, including particleSort to 92.88% and psDispParticles to 39.03%, plus an extab match.

key_files.role: Main decomp body: adds includes, local helper/cache structs, typed externs, particleSort implementation, and a large psDispParticles implementation.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: psdisp.c +1456/-7
diff.diff: psdisp.c include/type/global changes at top of file
diff.diff: adds particleSort and psDispParticles
issuecomment-4412862500: psdisp particleSort and psDispParticles progress

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for particleSort, adding baselib/forward.h and replacing UNK_T prototype with HSD_Particle* and HSD_Particle** types.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: psdisp.h +3/-1
diff.diff: psdisp.h changes at lines around diff 1502-1519

key_files.role: Struct layout refinement for HSD_psAppSRT: replaces padding from 0x6C to 0x9C with explicit f32 fields x6C through x98.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: psstructs.h +12/-1
diff.diff: psstructs.h changes at lines around diff 1520-1543

what_changed: Added baselib dependencies in psdisp.c for cobj, fog, lobj, mtx, psdisptev, state, and util, and switched from math.h to string.h with an explicit __fabsf alias.
Introduced local psdisp_ParticleSortBucket and psdisp_ParticleSortCache structs with STATIC_ASSERT size checks, plus Point3d as a Vec3 typedef.

Typed several psdisp-related extern globals, including hsd_804D0908 as HSD_Particle*[146], HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0 as a particle sort cache, HSD_PSDisp_804D7908 as HSD_Fog*, and HSD_PSDisp_804D7934 through HSD_PSDisp_804D7944 as GXColor.
Added particleSort, which buckets HSD_Particle linked lists by kind bits and caches sorted list heads/tails.

Added a large partial psDispParticles body using GX, HSD_CObj, HSD_Fog, psSetupTev state, matrix/projection cache math, particle color helpers, texture/form group locals, and many stack-shaped temporaries.
Updated psdisp.h so particleSort has a typed HSD_Particle* signature instead of UNK_T.
Expanded HSD_psAppSRT layout by replacing x6C_fill padding with twelve f32 fields x6C through x98.
Bot report recorded one new matched item for psdisp extab and improvements for psDispParticles, particleSort, .sdata2, extabindex, and .sdata.

smart_moves.text: Header was updated when the recovered particleSort body established a real HSD_Particle pointer signature, rather than keeping the old UNK_T declaration.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psdisp.h replaces UNK_T particleSort(s32,u8,UNK_T*,UNK_T*) with HSD_Particle* particleSort(s32,u8,HSD_Particle**,HSD_Particle**)

smart_moves.text: Known color globals were typed as GXColor and used through channel/TEV color setters, reducing UNK_T usage in the display state area.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: HSD_PSDisp_804D7934..804D7944 changed from UNK_T to GXColor
diff.diff: psDispParticles initializes GXColor fields and calls GXSetChanMatColor/GXSetChanAmbColor/GXSetTevColor

smart_moves.text: Temporary local structs with size assertions were used to document recovered layout assumptions for particle sorting bucket/cache data.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psdisp_ParticleSortBucket, psdisp_ParticleSortCache, and STATIC_ASSERT size checks

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body proves pointer roles, update the public header prototype in the same PR rather than leaving UNK_T/UNK_T* placeholders.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: particleSort implementation returns HSD_Particle* and writes HSD_Particle** outputs
diff.diff: psdisp.h particleSort prototype changed to HSD_Particle* and HSD_Particle**

decomp_lessons.lesson: For partial baselib decomp, typing globals and stack/cache shapes can unlock progress even when field names remain address-style or offset-style.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0 typed as psdisp_ParticleSortCache
diff.diff: HSD_PSDisp_804D7934..804D7944 typed as GXColor
diff.diff: HSD_psAppSRT x6C..x98 f32 fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: A large rendering function may need subsystem includes for camera, fog, lights, matrices, state, and TEV setup once generated calls are mapped to existing baselib APIs.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: added cobj.h, fog.h, lobj.h, mtx.h, psdisptev.h, state.h, util.h
diff.diff: psDispParticles calls HSD_CObjGetViewingMtx, HSD_FogSet, psSetupTevInvalidState, psSetupTevCommon, GX functions

naming_conventions.name: particleSort
naming_conventions.convention: Existing semantic camel-case style is preserved for particle sorting API.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: adds HSD_Particle* particleSort(...)
diff.diff: psdisp.h declares particleSort

naming_conventions.name: psdisp_ParticleSortBucket / psdisp_ParticleSortCache
naming_conventions.convention: New local helper structs use a psdisp_ prefix and semantic suffixes for recovered particle sorting layout.

diff.diff: typedef struct psdisp_ParticleSortCache

naming_conventions.name: x6C through x98
naming_conventions.convention: Unknown HSD_psAppSRT fields remain offset-style while type and layout are known but semantics are not proven.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psstructs.h replaces x6C_fill with f32 x6C..x98

naming_conventions.name: sp*, temp_r*, var_r* locals
naming_conventions.convention: Large psDispParticles body still contains stack/register-derived local names; this is evidence of partial decomp state, not an accepted naming standard.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psDispParticles declarations include sp8BC, sp7F4, temp/var-style names in particleSort

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used explicit __fabsf declaration and '#define fabsf __fabsf' after removing math.h.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: extern float __fabsf(float); #define fabsf __fabsf
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is a narrow matching tactic from the diff; no review discussion explains whether it should become general style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced a cache struct with padding to model a global area used both as particle sort tail storage and as f32 matrix/projection cache via casts.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psdisp_ParticleSortCache has x0_pad[0xAC] and HSD_Particle* xAC[17]
diff.diff: cache = (f32*) &HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0 in psDispParticles
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The mixed typed-struct and f32* access should be treated as partial recovered layout evidence, not final data ownership proof.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: particleSort uses duplicated bucket append blocks for buckets 0-7 and 8-15 rather than a natural loop.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: repeated (++temp_r3_4)->head blocks
diff.diff: repeated (++temp_r3_10)->head blocks
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded standards prefer natural loops unless verifier evidence requires unrolled code; this PR slice does not include author or reviewer justification for the unrolled shape.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Bot-reported progress was used to justify landing a partial large function: particleSort improved to 92.88% and psDispParticles to 39.03%.
evidence_refs: issuecomment-4412862500: particleSort +973 bytes, 0.00% to 92.88%
issuecomment-4412862500: psDispParticles +5655 bytes, 0.00% to 39.03%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The report is score evidence only; it is not full objdiff or local build detail for every source tactic.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update headers when recovered bodies prove signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2470 changes particleSort from an UNK_T-based header declaration to HSD_Particle* with HSD_Particle** output parameters alongside the recovered implementation, supporting the lesson that body recovery should update the owning header prototype.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psdisp.h particleSort prototype change
diff.diff: particleSort implementation signature

accepted_candidate_records.title: Offset-style field names are appropriate when layout is known but semantics are not
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2470 replaces HSD_psAppSRT padding with typed f32 fields named x6C through x98, preserving unknown semantics while documenting recovered type/layout.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psstructs.h x6C_fill replaced by f32 x6C..x98

source_update_candidates.title: psdisp.c contains particle display sorting and rendering code
source_update_candidates.text: src/sysdolphin/baselib/psdisp.c includes particleSort and psDispParticles and interacts with GX, HSD_Fog, HSD_CObj, matrices, and psdisp TEV setup.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psdisp.c adds particleSort and psDispParticles
diff.diff: added includes for cobj, fog, lobj, mtx, psdisptev, state, util
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scope fact for future psdisp and particle-rendering searches.

source_update_candidates.title: HSD_psAppSRT has f32 fields from 0x6C through 0x98 in this PR
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2470 replaces HSD_psAppSRT padding at 0x6C..0x98 with twelve f32 fields named x6C, x70, x74, x78, x7C, x80, x84, x88, x8C, x90, x94, and x98. Semantics are not established by the slice.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: psstructs.h replaces x6C_fill with f32 x6C..x98
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate data-sheet/layout update; keep offset-style names unless later evidence proves semantics.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not treat this PR's unrolled particleSort bucket appends as general style
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2470 contains repeated bucket append blocks in particleSort. Because the loaded slice lacks objdiff rationale for the unrolled form and current standards prefer natural loops, curator may want to use this as a cautionary historical example rather than an accepted pattern.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: repeated (++temp_r3_4)->head and (++temp_r3_10)->head blocks in particleSort
decomp_standards:natural-loops
decomp_standards:matching-tactics-need-evidence
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR is merged and scored, but the slice does not prove that unrolling was necessary; route as curator-reviewed caution only.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the __fabsf macro alias as a general standard; the slice shows it was used, but gives no rationale.
Do not infer human reviewer acceptance or requested changes; no human review comments are present.
Do not claim psDispParticles matched; bot report only shows 39.03% for that function.
Do not treat temp_r*, var_r*, and sp* local names as accepted naming convention; they indicate partial/generated-shaped decomp state in this slice.
